channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Three Pokémon Pokopia Upgrades You Should Buy Immediately
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Pokopia can be overwhelming sometimes. It’s one of the better examples of a life sim that streamlines its creative processes so anyone can pick it up and learn it quickly, but there are still a whole lot of minutiae, crafting recipes and materials, and upgrades to keep track of. To help you cut through the noise a bit as you’re starting out, here are a few upgrades you should try to prioritize as you rebuild the Pokémon world.

PP Up

You can buy a bottle of PP Up at the Pokémon Center’s PC several times over the course of the game as you find new areas and increase your environment level. These handy supplements will allow you to use your various attacks and abilities more often before you need to recharge by sleeping or eating food. 

Packing Tips

Much like PP Up, you’ll be able to purchase upgrades called Packing Tips multiple times throughout Pokopia. This gives you extra storage space in your bag, meaning you won’t have to rely as much on storage chests lying around the environment to hold your stuff. Once I’d upgraded a few times, storage space was basically a non-issue for me, and I almost always had whatever I needed for a certain quest or task on-hand.

Handy Bag

Speaking of item storage, the Handy Bag is one of those “this is so helpful it should have just been a baseline mechanic” sort of deals. This functions not only as an additional few slots of storage space, but can also be mapped as a hot bar to appear on the screen at all times, allowing you to quickly shuffle through a selection of items. This is perfect for building, gardening, or otherwise micromanaging a space, as you won’t have to constantly be opening up your inventory and searching for the one seed or tile you’re looking for.



https://kotaku.com/pokemon-pokopia-best-upgrades-items-handy-bag-space-2000677269
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Battlefield 6, The Best-Selling Game Of 2025, Reportedly Hit With Layoffs
item.content:encoded:
Battlefield 6 was the best-selling premium video game in 2025, according to Circana. That wasn’t enough, though, to save the people who worked on it from layoffs. Electronic Arts has reportedly laid off an unknown number of developers across Battlefield Studios’ teams, including workers at series creator Dice.

On March 9, IGN reported that Battlefield publisher EA had laid off an unconfirmed number of individuals and informed them that these cuts are part of a “realignment” across all of the studios that work on Battlefield 6. All four studios that make up Battlefield Studios—Dice, Criterion, Ripple Effect, and Motive Studios—will reportedly remain open and will continue to support Battlefield 6‘s live-service seasons and updates.

“We’ve made select changes within our Battlefield organization to better align our teams around what matters most to our community,” said EA in a statement. “Battlefield remains one of our biggest priorities, and we’re continuing to invest in the franchise, guided by player feedback and insights from Battlefield Labs.”

Battlefield 6 launched last October and quickly became one of the biggest games of the year. According to Circana, EA’s gritty military shooter outsold even Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 in 2025 to become the best-selling premium video game in the United States. But that seemingly wasn’t enough to ensure the people working on the game could keep their jobs.

As previously reported in 2025, EA spent over $400 million on Battlefield 6 and allegedly had some wild expectations for the game. Some devs told Ars Technica that EA wanted Battlefield 6 to bring in 100 million players. Battlefield 1, previously the most successful entry in the franchise, only hit around 30 million players. Workers told the outlet that EA’s goals seemed both unreasonable and near impossible. Those expectations might explain why, despite BF6‘s big, successful launch, EA is cutting workers. Another possible factor? That big Saudi deal.

EA is currently in the middle of preparing to be bought by Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund, Silver Lake Partners, and President Donald Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner’s Affinity Partners. As announced in September, the consortium is planning to buy EA for $55 billion, and shortly after announcing the deal, EA promised no “immediate changes” to its workforce. EA’s shareholders have already approved the deal. 

Update: 3/9/26, 1:00 p.m. ET: Added comment from EA about the layoffs.



https://kotaku.com/battlefield-6-reportedly-hit-with-layoffs-ea-dice-best-selling-game-2000677258
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon Players Are Working Together To Unlock The Shooter’s Final Level
item.content:encoded:
Before the weekend started, Bungie activated some mysterious terminals in its recently released extraction shooter Marathon. This kicked off an alternate reality game, and over the last 72+ hours, players and content creators across Twitch, Discord, Twitter, and Reddit have been working together to solve the puzzles with the hope that at the end of all this, Marathon’s final level will be unlocked.

On March 6, Bungie quietly activated previously offline computer terminals scattered around the first map in Marathon. These terminals looked and sounded like the ones found in Bungie’s original Marathon trilogy. And players quickly figured out that terminals led to other terminals, and if you visited them in the right order, you’d hear a message from an unknown voice that the community believes is Durandal, the psycho AI and main villain (?) from those older Marathon games. Intriguing!

👀 Durandal #Marathon

Credit: __meh pic.twitter.com/2BXVaTHiec

— Marathon Codec (@MarathonCodec) March 6, 2026

These terminals also led players to a website featuring scrambled camera feeds from a location named “Cyro Archive.” That’s the name of the previously announced “endgame” map of Marathon. Bungie later shared this website, and that’s when the online community puzzle-solving really began in earnest.

Players discovered that the different scrambled camera feeds on the Cryo Archive website had progress bars that changed every 15 minutes. But progress seemed to stall over the weekend, so Bungie dropped some hints in a new channel in the official Marathon Discord server. This helped guide players and creators to realize they would need to do a lot of terminal runs.

Eventually, players pieced together that if enough of them activated specific terminals on Marathon’s first map, Perimeter, during specific 15-minute intervals, they could fill the progress bars and unlock the cameras on the website, which led to more progress bars. Much of the effort to solve the puzzle has been organized by content creator and streamer MrRoflWaffles, who has even been able to even convince entire lobbies of Marathon players to chill out and work together to solve this ARG.

‼️NEW: FIRST LOOK AT CRYO ARCHIVE MAP LOCATIONS REVEALED VIA THE NEW ARG

– Cargo
– Control
– Index pic.twitter.com/0uNTr9IxeS

— Marathon Bulletin (@TauCetiGG) March 9, 2026

At midnight on Sunday, players were able to unlock all the cameras on the Cyro Archive website. And now…the community waits for Bungie to activate the next step in the ARG puzzle. That is likely going to involve terminals activating on Marathon’s other maps, which will let players decode more of the website.  According to dataminers, this is step three in a 13-step puzzle that is going to involve other websites, some of which have been found already, and more community cooperation.

The end result of all this hard work will (likely) be a new map to explore that will probably contain some big connections to the classic Marathon games. Plus, it will be a new place to get killed in a few minutes by some jerk with a sniper. And that’s fun.



https://kotaku.com/marathon-players-solve-next-step-cyro-archive-arg-bungie-puzzle-discord-terminals-2000677222
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
